response:
Short answer: azr-compliance-v3 performs Event Grid remediation via a backend-orchestrated API call into APIM (the APIM “ensure” endpoint) that can be driven one-off from the Event Grid Details page or as a server-side bulk remediation job. The bulk flow is implemented with dedicated Postgres tables and a timer worker rather than reusing the existing sync_queue.

Key points / flow:

- Trigger
  - Operators start remediation from the Event Grid Details UI (the page now explicitly separates deployment inventory from remediation coverage).
  - The frontend calls the Fleet backend remediation endpoint (previous browser-side designs were tried but the final flow is backend-mediated because Fleet requires application permission).

- Authentication / calling APIM
  - The backend acquires a Rackspace identity token (shared/fleet_event_grid_helpers.py calls shared.janus_helpers.get_rackspace_identity_token(auth_mode='identity') using RACKSPACE_USERNAME/RACKSPACE_PASSWORD) and sends that as Authorization: Bearer to APIM.
  - Earlier prototypes used ClientSecretCredential and MSAL in the browser; those were replaced because of application-permission requirements and credential handling in deployed environments.
  - The backend calls the APIM endpoint (example pattern: https://apim.mscloud.rackspace.com/v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription) and polls the returned statusUrl until the operation completes.

- Bulk remediation implementation
  - Dedicated Postgres-backed tables were added: event_grid_bulk_remediation_runs and event_grid_bulk_remediation_run_items.
  - A dedicated timer worker ProcessEventGridBulkRemediation runs every ~15 seconds and is guarded by its own advisory lock to ensure single active processor.
  - The bulk start endpoint snapshots the full deployable subscription set from the shared Event Grid coverage SQL (so it persists the exact items to remediate) and will reuse an active queued/running run if present (to avoid duplicate runs).

- Reliability and safeguards
  - The design intentionally does not reuse the old sync_queue and uses dedicated DB tables and a worker to manage retries/visibility and run state.
  - The backend polls statusUrl returned by APIM to determine completion.
  - There are safeguards in the API (e.g., validating the targeted subscription belongs to the URL customer and writing mismatch events) to avoid cross-customer mistakes.

- Observability / UI
  - GetEventGridDeployments now returns metadata.coverage (deployable_missing_event_grid_count, missing_monitoring_count, lists of deployable_subscriptions vs out_of_scope_subscriptions) so the UI can show explicit remediation candidates vs inventory.
  - The remediation action is surfaced on the Event Grid Details page and the frontend calls the Fleet backend remediation endpoint.

- Operational notes
  - There were production failures caused by missing environment variables in azr-fleet-platform-prod (AUTH_TENANT_ID / AUTH_ALLOWED_AUDIENCES); those were addressed in prior fixes.

If you want, I can diagram the exact sequence of API calls and DB writes for a single-subscription remediation and for a bulk run.

Added unit assertions to catch both regressions, reran ruff and focused pytest, rep", "2026-04-08: On 2026-04-08 in azr-compliance-v3, implemented Event Grid inventory scoping and Rackspace ownership filtering. SyncEventGrid no longer scopes by customer_subscriptions + monitoring service blocks alone; it now scopes to managed monitoring subscriptions by joining customer_subscriptions to lighthouse_delegations, requiring managed_by_tenant_id to equal the Rackspace Lighthouse tenant and delegation_state in active/succeeded. The sync still uses the single Rackspace Lighthouse credential and inte", "2026-04-08: On 2026-04-08 in azr-compliance-v3 planning, live Azure inspection showed the shared edge for governance/dev-governance is not codified in repo IaC. Application Gateway azr-waf-platform in resource group azr-fleet-platform-infra has one global WAF policy waf-apim attached (forceFirewallPolicyAssociation=true), shared listeners for governance/dev-governance/APIM/webhook, and path maps governance-path/dev-governance-path routing /policy-engine/v2/* to APIM pools and /* to frontend pools. WAF polic", "2026-03-23: On 2026-03-23 in azr-fleet-platform PR #67, three additional Event Grid review findings were validated and fixed in commit 5cd48c3. Fixes: targeted Event Grid DELETE now validates that the subscription belongs to the URL customer before starting the delete orchestration while still skipping monitoring-tier eligibility; ensure_event_subscription_single now writes a Cosmos EventGridSubscriptionCustomerMismatch event before returning a customer/subscription mismatch failure; orchestrate_onboarding ", "2026-03-23: On 2026-03-23 in fleet-platform-app, targeted Event Grid remediation API failures in prod had two independent causes. First, azr-fleet-platform-prod was missing AUTH_TENANT_ID and AUTH_ALLOWED_AUDIENCES, causing backend function auth to return 'JWT validation is not configured; AUTH_TENANT_ID/AZURE_TENANT_ID missing' and 503 Authentication service unavailable for http_eventgrid_subscription_targeted. Fix: set AUTH_TENANT_ID=570057f4-73ef-41c8-bcbb-08db2fc15c2b and AUTH_ALLOWED_AUDIENCES=api://da"]}
